regions.us-ports: Today Miami reports WNW 3kt winds, overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability; embarkation and shore excursions remain on schedule with light winds easing tendering and docking. Heat Advisory in effect for Port Everglades (heat index up to 108°F), Philadelphia (heat index up to 100°F), Los Angeles and Long Beach (temperature up to 104°F), and San Diego (temperature up to 85°F). Next 24–48 hours: Day 1 SSE 8kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, Day 2 S 13kt, drizzle, 20% rain probability for Port Everglades and Port Canaveral, and Day 3 S 12kt, drizzle, less than 10% rain probability for Tampa Bay.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today elevated heat with heat index reaching 103 degrees poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, Key West, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, Falmouth, Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná, with ESE winds at 7 knots and less than 10% rain probability supporting port operations and embarkation activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, SE winds increase to 10 knots on Day 2 while overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability persist across all ports, allowing continued normal tender operations and excursion scheduling. Beyond 48 hours, SE winds strengthen to 13 knots on Day 3 with drizzle developing and less than 10% rain probability maintaining, requiring minimal operational adjustments for cruise ship scheduling through the region.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today E 9kt winds, partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability support normal embarkation, disembarkation and shore excursions at San Juan. Next 24–48 hours Day 1: E 16kt winds, drizzle, less than 10% rain probability; Day 2: E 16kt winds, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability, affecting San Juan, St. Thomas and St. Croix and warranting extra caution during tendering. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 shows E 16kt winds, drizzle and less than 10% rain probability indicating persistent easterly breezes for St. Maarten, St. Kitts and Antigua and possible minor topside adjustments to gangway procedures.
regions.western-caribbean: Today Elevated Heat Day 2: Heat index reaching 100F will affect Cozumel, Costa Maya and Belize City and raise heat stress for outdoor shore excursions; Cozumel currently ENE 7kt, overcast, 14% rain probability. Next 24-48 hours, Cozumel and Roatan are forecast ENE 10kt with thunderstorms and 14% rain probability on Day 1, while Costa Maya and Belize City are forecast ENE 9kt with drizzle and 12% rain probability on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours, Day 3 trends show NE 8kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability across Cozumel and Costa Maya, supporting routine tendering and embarkation/disembarkation planning under lower wind speeds.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today elevated winds (22kt expected) are active for Aruba, Curacao and Bonaire; current conditions are E 19kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, and cruise tender operations at Aruba and Bonaire should expect choppy approaches and potential delays. Over the next 24-48 hours Day 1 shows E 22kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 shows E 22kt, drizzle, less than 10% rain probability, so Curacao and Bonaire should plan adjusted disembarkation timing and sheltered shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 shows E 19kt, thunderstorms, 38% rain probability and may affect Cartagena's outdoor shore excursions and on-pier gangway operations.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today E 9kt, clear skies, 11% rain probability will allow on-time embarkation and disembarkation at Barbados while supporting open-air shore excursions from the port. Over the next 24 to 48 hours E 15kt, drizzle, 32% rain probability may affect tendering and beach excursions for St. Lucia, Martinique and Dominica. Beyond 48 hours an ENE 16kt trend with drizzle and 10% rain probability indicates breezier deck conditions that could require modified outdoor programming aboard calls to Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent and Grenada.
regions.eastern-pacific: Today WNW winds at 10 knots with drizzle and a 30% rain probability may affect shore excursions and tendering operations across the region, particularly in Cabo San Lucas, Ensenada, and Puerto Vallarta. Over the next 24-48 hours, WNW winds remain steady at 10 knots with conditions improving to overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability by Day 2, allowing normal port operations and outdoor activities to resume. By Day 3, an elevated heat day with heat index reaching 102F will develop as SE winds shift to 8 knots and skies remain overcast with a 10% rain probability, requiring passengers to take precautions during extended time on deck or during midday shore excursions in Mazatlan, Manzanillo, and Huatulco.
regions.los-angeles: Today SE 3kt, foggy, less than 10% rain probability; reduced harbor visibility from fog will slow embarkation and disembarkation operations at Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach. Next 24–48 hours Day 1 SSE 9kt, foggy, less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 SSE 8kt, foggy, less than 10% rain probability will keep light SSE winds and may require adjusted docking alignment and tendering windows for Marina del Rey and Catalina Island. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 SSE 8kt, clear skies, less than 10% rain probability signals improving visibility and allows restoration of scheduled on-deck shore-excursion briefings and open-deck passenger movements.
regions.ensenada: Today light WSW 1kt winds, partly cloudy skies, and less than 10% rain probability support routine embarkation and disembarkation operations at Ensenada and Punta Banda, with minimal wind constraints for gangway and tender movements. Over the next 24 to 48 hours W 8kt winds with foggy conditions and less than 10% rain probability will affect visibility and could require delayed tendering or extended pilot transfer times for Ensenada, Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 shows WSW 9kt winds, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability, restoring favorable visibility for coastal transits and sightseeing launches to Islas Todos Santos while easing navigation through Ensenada approaches.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today WNW winds at 10 knots with drizzle and a 30% rain probability may affect shore excursions and tendering operations across Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, WNW winds persist at 10 knots with conditions improving to overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing normal port activities to resume. By day three, wind direction shifts to SE at 8 knots with 10% rain probability and continued overcast conditions, supporting routine embarkation and disembarkation procedures at all three ports.
regions.mazatlan: Today, NW winds at 10 kt with thunderstorms and 28% rain probability will support normal port operations at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, though a heat index reaching 102F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions and requires extended hydration breaks for crew. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, WSW winds at 5 kt with thunderstorms and 36% rain probability may affect embarkation and water-based activities across all three ports, while heat index values again reaching 102F will persist as the primary operational constraint. Beyond 48 hours, SE winds at 8 kt will accompany a 56% rain probability with thunderstorms, marking the most significant precipitation threat of the forecast period and likely affecting port schedules and excursion timing in the region.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today thunderstorms with 36% rain probability and SE winds at 9 kt may affect shore excursions throughout Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala, while heat index values reaching 104F pose a serious risk to passengers during outdoor activities. Over the next 24-48 hours, W winds at 8 kt on Day 2 combined with a 43% rain probability will continue to create unsettled conditions across all four ports, though embarkation and disembarkation operations should proceed with minor delays. Beyond 48 hours, ENE winds at 6 kt on Day 3 accompany an increasing 53% rain probability, which is expected to affect port operations and excursion scheduling across the region.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today E 2kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, so light easterly wind allows normal embarkation and disembarkation at Barcelona with minimal gangway restrictions. Over the next 24–48 hours Day 1 is forecast ESE 8kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, then Day 2 SE 9kt, foggy, less than 10% rain probability, when reduced visibility may delay shore excursions and tendering in Valencia and Palma de Mallorca. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 shows ESE 8kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, keeping light ESE winds for Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon and Gibraltar that permit routine navigation and scheduled port calls.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today elevated heat with a heat index reaching 100F will affect all ports from Marseille through Dubrovnik, with current SSW 5kt winds and partly cloudy skies limiting natural cooling during peak shore excursion hours across Naples, Civitavecchia, and Sardinia. Over the next 24-48 hours, heat index values climbing to 102F on Day 2 will persist with WSW 7kt winds and foggy conditions on Day 1 reducing visibility at embarkation points in Venice and Split, followed by SSW 8kt winds and partly cloudy skies on Day 2 as the heat index remains dangerously elevated. Beyond 48 hours, a heat index of 101F on Day 3 combined with SSW 8kt winds and foggy conditions will continue to challenge passenger comfort during outdoor activities, particularly at higher-elevation excursions in Dubrovnik and along the Corsican and Sardinian coastlines, with less than 10% rain probability persisting across all three days offering no relief.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today ELEVATED HEAT affecting Athens (Piraeus), Santorini and Mykonos; expect adjustments to embarkation timing, gangway rotations, and shaded-only shore excursions with NNE 6kt winds, clear skies, less than 10% rain probability. Next 24-48 hours will keep higher heat for Rhodes, Izmir and Haifa, affecting long-deck operations and water activities with Day 1 forecast NNE 8kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 forecast NNE 10kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today Juneau reports WNW 5kt winds, partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting berth operations and outdoor shore excursions in Juneau, Ketchikan and Skagway. Over the next 24–48 hours Juneau forecasts WNW 6kt winds with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, a pattern relevant to docking and canopy use in Juneau, Ketchikan and Skagway. Beyond 48 hours the forecast shifts to W 7kt with drizzle and 12% rain probability in Juneau, and compared with the earlier WNW 6kt guidance this small westward wind vector change could affect approach headings into Sitka and Icy Strait Point.
